full_text:
Precision oncology of SPAtial immune esCape mEchanisms in ovarian cancer

SPACE project – Aims & Main result updates
Ziqi, Andreas

Motivation & Aims

Approach: Use multi-modal profiling to (i) define tumor-intrinsic genomic states, (ii) quantify tissue-scale spatial immune architecture, and (iii) resolve local functional tumor/immune states. The, test whether architecture modes and state features explain outcomes beyond known clinical covariates.

Data
WES - define tumor-intrinsic genomic states 
Bulk RNA-seq - tumor-intrinsic programs associated with WES
tCycIF - quantify tissue-scale spatial immune architecture.
Xenium TMA - resolve local functional states at the interface and tumor core.
Public validation dataset
(Optional) Ex vivo experiment

Steps
Quantitatively analysis on TME features, identify baseline patterns and CNA-specific spatial modes.
Profile the functional states of tumor and immune cells underlying spatial modes (immune exclusive / enriched / inflammation). 
Find the states or programs that are relevant to prognosis. 

Analysis to-do list
1. Characterize and compare spatial TME across and within patients. Resolve the functional states underlying the resulting architecture modes. 
a: immune proximity to TSI; immune infiltration and densities. 
b: The infiltration of CD8T and the colocalization with myeloids (DCs, M1/M2). 
c: Spatial distribution and the states of CD4, Tregs & NK. 
d: The distribution of B cells. 
e: Tumor transcriptional changes relevant with oncogenic gene amplification

, M1/M2). 
c: Spatial distribution and the states of CD4, Tregs & NK. 
d: The distribution of B cells. 
e: Tumor transcriptional changes relevant with oncogenic gene amplification
2. Test whether the architecture modes and key spatial/state features explain clinical outcomes and therapy response beyond known factors.

Motivation: How do tumor-intrinsic genomic features shape the pre-established (PDS) tumor microenvironment (TME) in HGSC, relate to patient prognosis and therapeutic response?

Assumptions: 
Pre-established HGSC-TME has spatial immune architectures that can associate with prognosis. 
Tumor-intrinsic CNA states and downstream transcriptional programs condition or dominate spatial architectures, yielding distinct modes. 
Spatial multi-omics resolves functional immune & tumor states underlying new architectures and suggests therapeutic targets.

Key references

What we can learn from: 
Myeloid cell networks govern re-establishment of original immune landscapes in recurrent ovarian cancer https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ccell.2025.07.005 
Spatially organized multicellular immune hubs in human colorectal cancer
https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cell.2021.08.003 
Spatiotemporal architecture of immune cells and cancer-associated fibroblasts in high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/sciadv.adk8805 
Ovarian cancer mutational processes drive site-specific immune evasion https://doi.org/10.1038/s41586-022-05496-1 
Mapping spatial organization and genetic cell-state regulators to target immune evasion in ovarian cancer https://doi.org/10.1038/s41590-024-01943-5 
Spatial proteo-transcriptomic profiling reveals the molecular landscape of borderline ovarian tumors and their invasive progression (LGSC) https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ccell.2025.06.004 
ORION-FISH https://doi.org/10.1158/2159-8290.CD-26-0171 Spatial integration of protein and chromosomal states reveals early copy number changes and genotype-associated immune neighborhoods in serous ovarian cancer evolution
Multimodal Spatial Profiling Reveals Immune Suppression and Microenvironment Remodeling in Fallopian Tube Precursors to High-Grade Serous Ovarian Carcinoma https://doi.org/10.1158/2159-8290.CD-24-1366

Backgrounds:

Immune Suppression and Microenvironment Remodeling in Fallopian Tube Precursors to High-Grade Serous Ovarian Carcinoma https://doi.org/10.1158/2159-8290.CD-24-1366

Backgrounds: 
Cancer cell-intrinsic mechanisms driving acquired immune tolerance: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.immuni.2023.09.004 
Immune evasion in cancer: mechanisms and cutting-edge therapeutic approaches https://doi.org/10.1038/s41392-025-02280-1 
Crosstalk of T cells within the ovarian cancer microenvironment https://www.cell.com/trends/cancer/fulltext/S2405-8033(24)00190-0 
The distinct landscape of tumor immune microenvironment in homologous recombination deficient cancers https://doi.org/10.1186/s40364-025-00814-x 
Neoadjuvant PARPi or chemotherapy in ovarian cancer informs targeting effector Treg cells for homologous-recombination-deficient tumors https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cell.2024.06.013 
Genetics and anatomy sculpt immune-cell partners of ovarian cancer https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-022-04169-3 

https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-024-50137-y Timing of whole genome duplication is associated with tumor-specific MHC-II depletion in serous ovarian cancer
